content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 25,
1992
Vicki Kasad, City Clerk
City of Lake Elsinore
130 South Main St.
Lake Elsinore,
CA
92530
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
G-92-479
Dear Ms.
Kasad:
You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).’-!
You have asked whether a recipient committee formed and
reporting at the county level may circulate petitions to qualify a
city recall measure.
Your letter makes reference to a requirement
that petition circulators and signators must be registered to vote
in the city.
However, this requirement is contained in the
Elections Code (Elections Code Section 27035),
over which the
Commission has no jurisdiction.
There is nothing in the Political
Reform Act which would prohibit a county recipient committee from
engaging in recall activities at the city level.
You may wish to contact the Elections Division of the
Secretary of State’s office,
or the Attorney General,
for
assistance concerning provisions of the Elections Code.
If you have any questions concerning this letter, please
contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
*k
Carla Wardlow
Chief, Technical Assistance Division
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95812
•
(916) 322-5660

sections.question: Whether a recipient committee formed and reporting at the county level may circulate petitions to qualify a city recall measure.
sections.conclusion: There is nothing in the Political Reform Act which would prohibit a county recipient committee from engaging in recall activities at the city level.
sections.question_synthetic: May a county-level recipient committee engage in recall activities at the city level by circulating petitions to qualify a city recall measure?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that the Political Reform Act does not prohibit a county-level recipient committee from engaging in recall activities at the city level. However, voter registration requirements for petition circulators and signatories are governed by the Elections Code, which is outside the FPPC's jurisdiction.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Document references Elections Code provisions outside FPPC jurisdiction and recommends contacting the Secretary of State's Elections Division or Attorney General for guidance on those matters.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a recipient committee formed and reporting at the county level may circulate petitions to qualify a city recall measure.

CONCLUSION: There is nothing in the Political Reform Act which would prohibit a county recipient committee from engaging in recall activities at the city level.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission September 25, 1992 Vicki Kasad, City Clerk City of Lake Elsinore 130 South Main St. Lake Elsinore, CA 92530 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. G-92-479 Dear Ms. Kasad: You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’-! You have asked whether a recipient committee formed and reporting at the county level may circulate petitions to qualify a city recall measure. Your letter makes reference to a requirement that petition circulators and signators must be registered to vote in the city. However, this requirement is contained in the Elections Code (Elections Code Section 27035), over which the Commission has no jurisdiction. There is nothing in the Political Reform Act which would prohibit a county recipient committee from engaging in recall activities at the city level. You may wish to contact the Elections Division of the Secretary of State’s office, or the Attorney General, for assistance concerning provisions of the Elections Code. If you have any questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322—5662. Sincerely, *k Carla Wardlow Chief, Technical Assistance Division 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, g. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that the Political Reform Act does not prohibit a county recipient committee from circulating petitions to qualify a city recall measure, though Elections Code requirements regarding voter registration of circulators and signatories fall outside FPPC jurisdiction.
